message:
Add slice function changelog entry for list support

The slice function now supports list types in addition to strings,
enabling users to slice lists with begin, end, and stride parameters.
Negative stride values are now supported for reversing or stepping
backward through both strings and lists.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,17 @@
+---
+title: Slice function extended to support lists
+type: feature
+authors:
+  - mavam
+  - claude
+pr: 5819
+created: 2026-02-22T18:04:20.448605Z
+---
+
+The `slice` function now supports `list` types in addition to `string`. You can slice lists using the same `begin`, `end`, and `stride` parameters. Negative stride values are now supported for both strings and lists, letting you reverse or step backward through the data.
+
+Example usage with lists:
+
+- `[1, 2, 3, 4, 5].slice(begin=1, end=4)` returns `[2, 3, 4]`
+- `[1, 2, 3, 4, 5].slice(stride=-1)` returns the list in reverse order
+- `[1, 2, 3, 4, 5].slice(begin=1, end=5, stride=-2)` returns `[5, 3]`
